observations.recommendation: We recommended dropping from the books of accounts properties that are unserviceable and otherwise prepare the necessary adjusting entries relative to the properties dropped from the books of accounts for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements. Henceforth, we recommended compliance with:  Section 73 of PD No. 1445 on the requirement for the notice of loss and request for relief in connection with the loss of property caused by fire, theft, or other casualty or force majeure;  Section 125 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 79 of PD 1445 on the proper disposal procedures of unserviceable properties; and  COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 on the insurance coverage of insurable properties.
observations.reason: Other matters were prioritized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:  Municipal Treasurer to continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and re-file the request for write-off to COA;  Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present the Loans Receivable-Others and Due from NGOs/POs accounts in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and  Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to identify the particular account/s for which the collections of partial payments for Loans Receivable-Others were taken up and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to make the necessary adjusting entries for the partial collections of receivables recorded as Miscellaneous Income, and the Municipal Treasurer committed to continue exerting efforts to collect from the identified debtors.
observations.reason: No action. The Municipality failed to collect from identified debtors because sending of demand letters was deferred due to the pandemic. The request for write-off was also not re-filed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-General Services Officer
and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other and complete the
reconciliation of the affected PPE accounts and make the necessary adjustments in
their respective records, if warranted. Henceforth, ensure that PPELCs for each
category of PPE are prepared and maintained by the Municipal Accountant in
compliance with Section 114 of MNGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-General Services Officer
and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other and complete the
reconciliation of the Inventory accounts and make the necessary adjustments in their
respective records, if warranted. Henceforth, ensure that stock cards for all types of
inventories and SLCs for each commodity/stock of inventory are prepared and
maintained by the ICO-General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant,
respectively, in compliance with Section 114 of MNGAs for LGUs, Volume 1.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Municipal Treasurer to continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified
debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular
No. 2016-005 and re-file the request for write-off to COA;
 Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in
order to present the Loans Receivable-Others and Due from NGOs/POs accounts
in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the
accounts in the financial statements; and
 Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to identify the
particular account/s for which the collections of partial payments for Loans
Receivable-Others were taken up and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit
the balance of P171,534.44 to the concerned source agency pursuant COA Circular
No. 94-013 so that it can be used to finance other projects or by other agencies
needing assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-General Services Officer to continue and complete the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team. Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted physical count on PPE and submitted the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Continuous Partially Implemented although undertaken, was incomplete. The variance of P11,498,576.74 has been reduced to P6,433,830.88. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 22 to 23, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  require the ICO-General Services Officer to complete the physical count of inventories and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Audit Team;  require the ICO-General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and maintain stock cards for supplies;  require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock of inventory and record purchases of supplies/inventories initially as an asset account and recognize the expense once the inventories are consumed/distributed.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-General Services Officer submitted the RPCI to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant traced all the transactions under the inventory accounts and discovered that the Office Supplies, Construction Materials, Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies, Agricultural and Marine Supplies, and Other Supplies and Materials Inventory accounts have balances amounting to P239,374.61, P363,921.00, P296,545.00, P2,103,763.00 and P2,268,386.48 respectively, that were carried in the books of accounts for more than 10 years but were not included in the yearly RPCI, thus resulting to a variance in the total amount of P5,271,990.09. He then committed to make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: The balances mentioned by the Municipal Accountant were considered by the Audit Team in checking whether the amounts, if added to the RPCI balances, will make the two records reconciled. The discrepancies noted in the Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies, Agricultural and Marine Supplies, and Other Supplies and Materials Inventory accounts have decreased while differences in the Office Supplies and Construction Materials Inventory accounts have increased. Nevertheless, the total variance was trimmed down to P4,524,212.84. Likewise, we wish to remind the Management to continue to complete the reconciliation of the Inventory accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor continue securing the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the 17 parcels of land and cause the immediate titling of the same in the name of the Municipality by continually coordinating with other agencies concerned with the transfer of title contemplated to be undertaken, to avoid possible third party claims that may arise in the future and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted sufficient evidence to establish the existence, valuation and ownership of Land account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to trace the causes of the non-reconciliation and make the appropriate adjustments for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements which is required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant traced the cause of the non-reconciliation and made the appropriate adjustments under Journal Entry Voucher No. 401-2021-03-0657.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  instruct the ICO-General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards for each inventory item, including those that are COVID-19-related, conduct periodic physical count of inventories, and submit the corresponding RPCI complete with necessary information and correct classification of inventory accounts;  require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SLCs for the inventories, record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially as an asset account, and recognize the expense in the proper accounting period once the inventories are consumed/distributed;  direct the Municipal Officials and Employees to refrain from procuring goods on reimbursement basis and adhere to the procurement procedures provided in GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 in order to prevent abuses and corruption in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-General Services Officer submitted the RPCI to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant now records the purchases of supplies/ inventories initially as an asset account and recognizes the expense in the proper accounting period once the inventories are consumed or distributed. Municipal Officials and Employees were directed to adhere to the procurement procedures provided in GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.reason: No SLCs were maintained because of other matters that were prioritized by the Municipality. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 23 to 25, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation on June 1, 2021; otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit and (b) properly charge the amount of P507,150.00 against the available MOOE allotment pursuant to Item 7.0 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 and revert/transfer such amount to PS allotment and prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to record the hazard pay to JO personnel to the MOOE account for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1. Henceforth, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and charged to the proper allotment class.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted the required documents to the Audit Team for evaluation on June 1, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts for stale checks upon return/surrender of the same for cancellation and replacement pursuant to existing regulations on stale checks. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to give notice to the payees or holders of checks at least one (1) month before the checks become stale.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the affected accounts per JEV No. 101-2021-08-3300.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to allocate enough time for the reconciliation of the affected accounts for their fair presentation in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS. We also recommended that henceforth, all contributions and taxes withheld from its employees, suppliers and contractors are remitted intact to the respective government agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made an inquiry to the BIR for any outstanding unremitted balance. Remittances were made in January 2022.
observations.reason: There are still unreconciled/unremitted prior months' and years' contributions in the Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to continue exerting efforts to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the existing regulations by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer assist the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer by providing the necessary supporting documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for property insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Based on the report submitted by the General Services Office, an updated report was indicated that insured properties totaling P4,643,540.00 were insured in CY 2021. The ICO-Municipal General Services Officer submitted an updated report and provided the necessary supporting documents to the ICO-Municipal Services Officer for some properties.
observations.reason: Only properties in the amount of P4,643,540.00 were insured in CY 2021. The remaining properties were not insured to date because of budget limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Budget Officer to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund through adequate planning of the PPAs to be procured and/or implemented during the year so that all the programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries might be deprived of the maximum benefits that could be derived from the development projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund and the utilization reached 84.91 percent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Officials and the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed formats of the LDRRMFIP and Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund, respectively, as provided in the same COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant already prepared and submitted the monthly reports to the Audit Team. The MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant now using the prescribed formats of the LDRRMFIP and Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to trace the causes of the non-reconciliation and make the appropriate adjustments for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.reason: Implementation is taking some time because the Accounting Office does not know yet the exact cause of the non-reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts. Reiterated under audit observation no. 6, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the accounts with the help of additional personnel in the Accounting Office who may be borrowed from another Office and work on this within an allocated time for the reconciliation of the affected accounts and consider the use of Enhanced Electronic National Government Accounting System (eNGAS) in recording transactions of the Municipality. We also recommended that all contributions and taxes withheld from its employees, suppliers and contractors are remitted intact to the respective government agencies.
observations.reason:
There was no information on the availability of personnel to help in the Accounting Office, and as regards the use of eNGAS, this did not materialize because when the Municipality requested for the training on the use and functionalities of the eNGAS the training on this was put on hold.
We also recommended that all contributions and taxes withheld from its employees, suppliers and contractors are remitted intact. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 10, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to continue and complete the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team. Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to complete the physical count of
inventories and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Office of the Auditor with
complete necessary information and correct classification of inventory accounts
and maintain stock cards for supplies;
• require the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant in the reconciliation of the inventory accounts, investigate and adjust
any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct inventory
account balances required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each
commodity/stock and record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially as an
asset account and recognize the expense account once the inventories are
consumed/distributed for proper matching of asset and expense accounts in
accordance with IPSAS 12 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of
these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit
to the Audit Team the documentation to support the validity and propriety of the
dropping from the books of accounts PPE worth P4,258,639.91, otherwise prepare the
necessary adjusting entries relative to the properties dropped from the books of
accounts for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
Henceforth, we recommended compliance with:
• Section 73 of PD 1445 on the requirement for the notice of loss and request for relief
in connection with the loss of property caused by fire, theft, or other casualty or force
majeure;
• Section 125 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 79 of PD 1445 on the
proper disposal procedures of unserviceable properties; and
• COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 on the insurance coverage of all insurable
properties with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order to
indemnify or compensate the Government for any damage to, or loss of, its
properties due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other casualty.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in securing the
Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the
supporting tax declaration of the 17 parcels of land and cause the immediate titling of
the same in the name of the Municipality by continually coordinating with other
agencies concerned with the transfer of title contemplated to be undertaken, to avoid
possible third party claims that may arise in the future and for the fair presentation of
the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS
1.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified
debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No.
2016-005 and re-file the request for write-off to the Commission on Audit; and
• Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order
to present the Loans Receivable-Others and Due from NGOs/POs accounts in the
financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of
COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the
financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to identify the particular account/s for which
the collections of partial payments for Loans Receivable-Others were taken up and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to trace the causes of the non-
reconciliation and make the appropriate adjustments for the fair presentation of the
accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards for
each inventory item, including those that are COVID-19-related, conduct periodic
physical count of inventories, and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Audit
Team complete with necessary information and correct classification of inventory
accounts;
• require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SLCs for the inventories, record the
purchases of supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate asset account, and
recognize the expense account once the inventories are consumed/distributed in
accordance with IPSAS 12; and
• direct the Municipal Officials and Employees to refrain from procuring goods on
reimbursement basis and adhere to the procurement procedures provided in GPPB
Circular No. 01-2020 in order to prevent abuses and corruption in the procurement
process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to (a)
submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation
otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit and (b) properly charge
the amount of P507,150.00 against the available MOOE allotment pursuant to Item 7.0
of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 and revert/transfer such amount to PS allotment
and prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to record the hazard pay to JO personnel
to the MOOE account for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statement
in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

Henceforth, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with
complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and charged to
the proper allotment class.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust
the affected accounts for stale checks upon return/surrender of the same for
cancellation and replacement pursuant to existing regulations on stale checks.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
to give notice to the payees or holders of checks at least one (1) month before the
checks become stale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to continue and complete the physical inventory of properties and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team. Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Conducted physical inventory work already initiated by her predecessor by particularly those items of PPE under Machinery and Equipment. Both the Accounting Office and the General Services Office coordinated with each other to have their records reconciled. However, verification of the report showed that there are still items of PPE per RPCPPE that do not match with accounting records, thus unreconciled. From a variance of P264,920,516.09 in CY 2019, it is worthy to note that this has been reduced to P11,498,576.74 in CY 2020.
observations.reason:
Non-reconciliation of the RPCPPE and Accounting records due to the sudden death of the former General Services Officer which resulted to a material variance of P264,920,516.09, contrary to Sections 124 and 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I and affected the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements which is not in accordance with Paragraph 27 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 1.
With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in securing the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the 11 parcels of land and cause the immediate titling of the same in the name of the Municipality by continually coordinating with other agencies concerned with the transfer of title contemplated to be undertaken, to avoid possible third party claims that may arise in the future and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Titling for one parcel of land in the amount of P1,035,300.00 has been processed and the Municipality is just waiting for the release of the title.
observations.reason: Titling of some lots was not facilitated due to incomplete documentary requirements and the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to complete the physical count of inventories and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Office of the Auditor with complete necessary information and correct classification of inventory accounts and maintain stock cards for supplies; 
• require the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the reconciliation of the inventory accounts, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct inventory account balances required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock and record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially as an asset account and recognize the expense account once the inventories are consumed/distributed for proper matching of asset and expense accounts in accordance with IPSAS 12 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the RPCI to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Unable to complete the physical count of all inventories due to the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic and because the person assigned to do the task is a newly hired employee who assumed office on February 17, 2020. Not Implemented complete the reconciliation due to the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic and the person assigned to do the task assumed office only on February 17, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and re-file the request for write-off to the Commission on Audit; and
• Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present the Loans Receivable-Others and Due from NGOs/POs accounts in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to identify the particular account/s for which the collections of partial payments for Loans Receivable-Others were taken up and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: Exerted extra effort and was able to collect from SEAP/ Tulong-Puhunan Program debtors. In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account and Due from NGOs/POs account in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Collections made were not properly recorded under the Loans Receivable-Others account because the personnel preparing the collection report is not familiar with the accounts to be used.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate her cash advances, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer submitted the liquidation reports to the Accounting Office.
Proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period is ensured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the recorded interest expenses and consequently adjust the computation and recording of the assets' depreciation expense.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The brand-new heavy equipment acquired by the Municipality is not considered as "qualifying asset" and therefore, the interest expenses shall not be capitalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts for stale checks upon return/surrender of the same for cancellation and replacement pursuant to existing regulations on stale checks. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to give notice to the payees or holders of checks at least one (1) month before the checks become stale.
observations.managementAction: Stale checks contained in the CY AAR have been adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to continue exerting efforts to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the existing regulations by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to assist the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer by providing the necessary supporting documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for property insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Registered and insured 14 units of Motor Vehicles which were submitted together with the AAPSI. Verification revealed that out of the 14 units of Motor Vehicles, only three pertain to properties reported in CY 2019 AAR with an aggregate amount of P5,789,086.88 while the remaining 11 represent those properties acquired in CY 2020 or in previous years but were not included in last year’s report. The balance of properties not covered with insurance per CY 2019 AAR have been reduced to P83,774,083.55 after deducting the amount of the three duly insured properties. However, review of the PPE accounts and the submitted List of Heavy Equipment, Light Vehicles and Other Machineries of the General Services Office showed additional items not covered with insurance raising the total to P90,868,303.07.
observations.reason: The ICO-General Services Officer and the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant were not able to gather the supporting documents required by the GSIS due to the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic. Management commented that due to limited budget, they are prioritizing the insurance coverage of newly acquired insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Budget Officer to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund through adequate planning of the Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) to be procured and/or implemented during the year so that all the programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Reduced the CY 2019 unutilized balance to P6,924,367.66 by the end of CY 2020 despite general delays brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to optimally utilize appropriations in CY 2020 due to the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic and unfavorable weather conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate the regular transfer and proper recording of the unutilized balance of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Reduced the CY 2019 unutilized balance and transferred the Local Disaster Risk Management Fund to the Trust Fund. The unutilized balance of Local Disaster Risk Management Fund for Trust Liability-DRRM in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to expedite the completion of the reconciliation of the property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy.
observations.reason:
Non-completion of the RPCPPE due to the sudden death of the former General Services Officer.
With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to exert extra effort to look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the 11 lots and cause the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality, and for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason:
Incomplete documentary requirement for the titling of lots.
With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the reconciliation of the accounts and remit whatever balance due the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices.
observations.reason: Not given preferential attention.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to provide the necessary supporting documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for property insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Sudden death of the former General Services Officer.
With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-General Services Officer to
continue the work already initiated by her predecessor by completing the physical
inventory of properties and submitting the corresponding RPCPPE to the Office of the
Auditor. Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts,
investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the
correct PPE balances required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue
exerting extra effort in securing the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry
of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the 11 lots and cause the immediate
titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality by continually coordinating with other
agencies concerned with the transfer of title contemplated to be undertaken, to avoid possible
third party claims that may arise in the future and for the fair presentation of the Land account
in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the ICO-General Services Officer to continue the work already initiated by
her predecessor by completing the physical count of inventories and submitting
the corresponding RPCI to the Office of the Auditor with complete necessary
information;
• require the completion of the reconciliation of the inventory accounts, investigate
and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct
inventory account balances required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies/ inventories
initially as an asset account and recognize the expense account once the
inventories are consumed/distributed for proper matching of asset and expense
accounts in accordance with IPSAS 12.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the
required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and refile the request for
write-off to the Commission on Audit.

In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should set up the provision for Allowance for
Impairment in order to present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account and Due from
NGOs/POs in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the account
in the financial statements required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately liquidate her cash advances, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that
all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to allow the granting of
succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances
within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations and
for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert
effort to trace the causes of the non-reconciliation and make the appropriate
adjustments for the fair presentation in the financial statements as required under
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the recorded interest expenses
and consequently adjust the computation and recording of the assets’ depreciation
expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust
the affected accounts for stale checks upon return/surrender of the same for
cancellation and replacement pursuant to existing regulations on stale checks.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
to give notice to the payees or holders of checks at least one (1) month before the
checks become stale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. General Service Officer to:
• conduct the physical count of all inventories
• prepare and submit RPCI to the immediately the Audit Team.
• maintain complete stock cards
b. Municipal Accountant to:
• prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC)
c. General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records regularly; and
d. Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the affected accounts.
observations.reason: With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit Report.

observations.managementAction: Exerted extra effort to collect from the identified debtors.
observations.reason: Preferential attention.

observations.managementAction: Reconciled the accounts.
observations.reason: With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to immediately submit their liquidation reports to the Accounting Office, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the NFUCC coordinate with the Office of the Human Resource Management before the approval of loan to ensure that the employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum of P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided under Section 48 of the GAA for FY 2018.

Offices were already coordinating on the approval of loans.
Collections were promptly remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component Barangays promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Collections were promptly remitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the NFUCC coordinate with the Office of the Human Resource Management before the approval of loan to ensure that the employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum of P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided under Section 48 of the GAA for FY 2018.
Henceforth, see to it that the mandatory monthly net take home pay provided in the GAA is strictly observed yearly.
observations.managementAction: Mandatory monthly net take home pay was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider hiring of additional technical personnel in the Office of the Municipal Engineer to prepare DED and/or POW subject to availability of funds and CSC rules and regulations so that all the programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Additional four technical persons were hired during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the PPAs listed in the LDRRMPs thru the MDRRMO to monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the PPAs listed in the LDRRMP to ensure the full implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs;
• submit explanation on the incurrence of operating and administrative expenses charged to LDRRMF for P615,618.00 and refrain from using the LDRRMF for PPAs that are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and
• require the MDRRMO to coordinate between the Budget, Accounting and Treasury Office for the early transfer of unutilized balance of DRRMF; and
• instruct the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Reports for DRRM Funds to enable the Team to conduct timely audit of MDRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction: Accomplishment Reports for DRRM Funds were already submitted.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
Not given preferential attention.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the General Services Officer to expedite the completion of the reconciliation of the
property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE
balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
exert extra effort to look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the
Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the 11 lots and
cause the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality, and for the
fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. General Service Officer to:
 conduct the physical count of all inventories of the Municipality;
 prepare and submit immediately the inventory reports; and
 maintain complete stock cards
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC);
c. General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the
records regularly; and
d. Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the affected
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the
required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and submit the
request for write-off to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
trace the causes of the non-reconciliation and make the appropriate adjustments
for the fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to
immediately submit their liquidation reports to the Accounting Office, and the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the
end of the year and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance only when
the previous one is fully liquidated.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MHRMO to use the
PhilHealth Electronic Premium Remittance System in the computation of monthly
contribution during payroll and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper
recording and monitoring of account balances are observed and the necessary
adjustments are effected to reflect the correct balances for the fair presentation of
the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component Barangays
promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section
36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the NFUCC coordinate with the Office
of the Human Resource Management before the approval of loan to ensure that the
employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum allowed
take home pay of P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided under Section 48 of the GAA for
FY 2018.

Henceforth, see to it that the mandatory monthly net take home pay provided in the
GAA is strictly observed yearly.

Corrected the erroneous deposit made. Monitor the bank balances. Depreciation are accurately. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 7, Part II of this Report.
observations.managementAction: Corrected the erroneous deposit made. Monitor the bank balances. Depreciation are accurately. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 7, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Erroneous recording and computation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to prepare and update the ledger cards and property cards for all types of properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: Started the reconciliation of the RPCPPE. The reconciliation is ongoing.
observations.reason: Non-completion of reconciliation of RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal Assessor to exert extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: Seven out of the 18 lots were already titled in the name of the Municipality. Titling of additional four lots were already in process. Still gathering documents for the remaining seven untitled lots.
observations.reason: Still gathering documents for the remaining seven untitled lots.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO) to: • conduct the physical count of all inventories of the Municipality; • prepare and submit immediately the inventory reports; and • maintain complete stock cards b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC); c. MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records regularly; and d. Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Exerted extra effort to collect from those identified debtors. For inventories: Submitted the RPCI to the Audit Team. However, sets of stock card and SLC are still not maintained; reconciliation was not facilitated and adjustment was not made. The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: The submitted RPCI was not a result of physical count; inventories submitted by each department; stock cards and SLC still not maintained; reconciliation and adjustment not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry for the transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the amount of P5,429,244.02 and the Municipal Treasurer to effect the transfer of the said amount from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Transfer of unspent LDRRMF for the year 2017 were already made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors; and for receivables without identified debtors and without documentation request from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of these receivables following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Exerted extra effort to collect from those identified debtors. Wrote a letter dated February 2, 2018 requesting authority to write-off under COA Circular No. 2016-005. Gathering of documents required for write-off still ongoing; demand letters were sent again to last known addresses.
observations.reason: Returned to the Municipality due to lack of supporting documents required. Lack of monitoring of receivables.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the account to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund pursuant to Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant is advised to recognize liability only when goods and services are accepted and rendered and with suppliers/creditors bills.
observations.managementAction: Reverted the account to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances to the Accounting Office, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated.
observations.managementAction: Liquated all the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer amounting to P1,271,800.00 and the Advances to Officers and Employees amounting to P103,780.00. Remaining cash advances granted to Mr. Wilson P. Rutaquio totaling P85,176.36 were still not liquidated. The Municipal Mayor commented that the remaining balance of cash advance will be deducted in the payroll of the employee.
observations.reason: Cash advances granted to Mr. Wilson P. Rutaquio were still not liquidated; some balances remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of Due from Other Funds with its reciprocal account Due to Other Funds and prepare necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Identified some of the reconciling items and prior year's items which effected the variance. Reverted the erring entries and effected adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Erroneous recording; unidentified reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the In-Charge (ICO) – Municipal Engineer to integrate the computation of Construction Safety and Health Program in the computation of construction cost of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality and require the contractors to submit Construction Safety and Health Program duly-approved by DOLE upon signing of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: The cost of CSHP is being integrated as separate line item in the computation of construction cost of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality. The Contractors are being required to submit their CSHP duly-approved by the DOLE upon signing the contracts. The Municipal Mayor commented that the Contractors are being required to submit Construction Safety and Health Program duly-approved by DOLE upon signing of the contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now remitting on a quarterly basis as of each quarter. The Municipal Mayor committed to ensure POWs are ready in the first quarter so that bidding may commence immediately and projects be completed before start of rainy seasons.
observations.reason: Prior year's implemented 20% Development Fund projects were not optimally utilized in 2018 due to lack of technical personnel to prepare DED/POW.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to promptly remit the RPT share to the component Barangays within five days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the recommendation. The Municipality is now remitting on a quarterly basis as of each quarter.
observations.reason: Unaware of the prescribed period; previously remitted once a year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete set of subsidiary ledgers for all economic enterprises operated by the Municipality and prepare the required Financial Statements and supporting schedules in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 105 and 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The complete sets of subsidiary ledgers were prepared by the Municipality and the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit and disclose the reconciliation either on the face of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with paragraph 21 of PPSAS 1 and paragraph 47 of PPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of SCBAA were disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the accounts and remit whatever balance due the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already made and balances were already remitted to the agencies concerned. The Municipal Mayor commented that they will consider hiring of additional personnel for the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel to trace the cause of unreconciled prior years balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor commented that they will require all accountable officer concerned to communicate with the GSO for the processing of their application for property insurance. Only some properties insured previously; due to absence of documents required by GSIS some were not insured. With new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No.11, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Due to absence of documents required by the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees or holder of checks one (1) month before they become stale so that concerned payees will be aware that these checks will no longer be honored by the bank after six months from the date of their issuance.


We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books of the affected accounts whenever stale checks are discovered, particularly during the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.reason:
Claimant/Payee has no reference materials presented. (e.g. ID)
Reiterated under observation no. 5, Part II of this report.




No adjustment was made. Reiterated under observation no. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the actual amounts presented in the financial statements; and disclose the said reconciliation on the face of the SCBAA or in the notes to financial statements in compliance with Section 47 of the PPSAS 24, to enable the Municipality to better discharge its accountability obligations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still failed to prepare and disclose the reconciliation of SCBBA. Reiterated under observation no. 10, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to conduct regular general revision of assessment to update current and previous taxable parcels for valuation and tax purposes in compliance with Section 219 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and allocating funds for the purpose.
observations.reason: The proposed schedule of Market Value for General Revision is still in the process of validation before the approval of BLGF Region IV-A and the Provincial Sanggunian.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to prepare corresponding ledger cards and property cards for all types of properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors; and for receivables without identified debtors and without documentation and collectability of these receivables is nil, request from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of these receivables following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005 may be filed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are monitored and properly liquidated at the end of the year in accordance with the above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the documents to support the unaccounted balance of P743,105.73 pursuant to the provisions of Section 59 of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees or holders of checks issued one (1) month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance.
We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books of the affected accounts whenever stale checks are discovered particularly during the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to review and compare her cashbook entries with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned Department Heads involve in the processing of payments to ensure that near maturing obligations are handled with utmost care to avoid the unnecessary penalties; and to refund the amount of P68,188.49 for the penalties for late payments. Payment of electricity and other basic utilities shall be given priority and shall be made on or before its due date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning so that all its programmed development  projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of selling CTCs to its deputized Barangay Treasurers and refund the amounts collected from Barangays representing the payments made for the costs of CTCs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit and disclose the reconciliation either on the face of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with paragraph 21 of PPSAS 1 and paragraph 47 of PPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Bids and Awards Committee and the General Services Officer to procure commonly used supplies and equipment from the DBM-PS in conformity with the above cited Administrative Order and DBM Circular Letter   No. 2013-14 to support the government’s policy for transparency in government transactions and efficiency in procurement processes; and
BAC to prepare and submit the APP-CSE to DBM pursuant to DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-16.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit the RPT share totalling P127,435.44 to the remaining Barangays.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to promptly remit the RPT share  to component Barangays within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement voucher for the cash advances totaling P200,000.00 for post audit with written justification/explanation for the delay and submit the corresponding liquidation reports to the COA ICFAU.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going government projects /programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year. Likewise, submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above-cited regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the establishment of a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile the accounts and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for the transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the amount of P5,599,143.86 and the Municipal Treasurer to effect the transfer of the said amount  from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.

observations.managementAction: Located some supporting documents to establish subsidiary ledgers. Sent demand letters to identified debtors. Settled the amount of P32,000.00 thru salary deductions
observations.reason:
Some debtors did not accept/receive the demand letters, some are dead, some migrated to other places and others refused to acknowledge the loan.
Reiterated under observation no. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the accountable officers concerned;
require the Accountable Officers to liquidate immediately their cash advances; and
require the Municipal Treasurer to effect salary deduction in case of failure of the accountable officers to liquidate their outstanding cash advances, in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: All cash advances has been liquidated except for the P21,612.00 of General Services Officer which are being liquidated thru salary deduction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the:
list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2015; and
monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.reason:
No submitted report.


Due to demographic constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards  Committee to comply with the requirement on the submission of perfected contracts  and purchase orders irrespective of amount and their supporting documents within five (5) working days from issuance thereof and the General Services Officer to submit copies of inspection reports of deliveries of goods and services within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable timely review and validation by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: There were delays in the submission due to demographic constraints.

observations.managementAction: Copy of daily attendance sheet were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement the Development Projects lined-up for the year to the optimum as provided for in Section 5 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to ensure maximum and immediate benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Various development projects were already implemented.
observations.reason: Some development projects lined-up for implementation needs additional funding and reprogramming.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee to send invitation to observers in all stages of the procurement in accordance with the above cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: Observers are being invited in all stages of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to justify the non-posting of the notices of awards in the PhilGEPS for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects totaling P38,037,235.63 and strictly adhere to the transparency provision cited above.
observations.managementAction: Some of the Notice of Awards are already posted.
observations.reason: Slow internet connection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to furnish the Audit Team copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds received from  the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in Item 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to determine whether the funds were utilized in  accordance with the specified purpose.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the MOA from the DA-RFO4A and PENRO for construction of Magsikap Bridge and Production of 10,000 quality seedlings (preferably endemic forest and fruit bearing trees)
observations.reason: Not all funds from National Government Agencies are supported with MOA or Trust Agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the balances of PDAF totaling P5,007,595.00 to the account of the National Treasury, pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision.
observations.managementAction: Deposited the balance of P5,007,595.00 to the National Treasury on May 19, 2016 (OR No. 8204742, DV No. 100-2016-05-2371 and Check No. 543961 dated May 18, 2016)

observations.recommendation: We also recommended the refund of the excess payment of P531,238.76.
observations.managementAction: Settled the amount of P487,282.70. With NSSDC
observations.reason: NFD and COE were   issued for the remaining balance of P44,030.54
